steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content:
The phrase “Her work challenges conventional notions of race, gender, history, and [X]” is an independent clause.

The phrase “she is credited with expanding the horizons of conceptual photographic art” is also an independent clause.

These two complete thoughts appear consecutively and must be properly joined.

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content:
A comma plus a coordinating conjunction joins two independent clauses.

Choice D (memory, and) provides exactly this—the comma and the “and” that properly connects the two clauses.

Choices A, B, and C fail to provide the correct punctuation: A creates a comma splice (comma only), while B and C create run-on sentences (missing the comma, the conjunction, or both).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
